title: OpenSearch Indexing Best Practices for Large Content Repositories
subtitle: How to structure crawled documents for search, analytics, and reindexing

content.headline: OpenSearch Indexing Best Practices for Large Content Repositories
content.body: This article explains how to design a robust document structure for OpenSearch indexing. It covers metadata modeling, nested objects, enrichment fields, timestamps, validation, and index lifecycle considerations. The goal is to produce a search-friendly and analytics-friendly JSON structure that can be reliably validated before ingestion.
content.summary: A practical guide for modeling and validating rich web documents before indexing them into OpenSearch.

paragraphs.text: Introduction to document modeling for search systems.

paragraphs.text: How nested structures improve organization while keeping schema discipline.
